_note: Pouch Radar v1 — a curated, dated snapshot. STRENGTHS (mg/pouch), FLAVOUR style/counts and PACK COUNTS are REAL, curated from public manufacturer product pages + the free Haypp Nicotine Pouch Report 2025 + reputable retailer/comparison listings (each row carries a specSource + specConfidence; UK = HIGH, SE/DK = MED). Per-can PRICES are now also REAL: an owner-cleared one-time price snapshot (2026-06-17) read live single-can list prices per market. UK prices via nicotine-pouches.org/Haypp UK, DK prices via din-ecigaret.dk, and SE prices via Haypp SE were each INDEPENDENTLY cross-verified against a second shop (UK +Snus Vikings/Two Wombats; DK +gejser-ecigaret.dk/kioskenrodbyhavn.dk; SE +Nettotobak, exact 1-pack matches) → all priceVerified:true, HIGH. (SE's independent re-verify originally died on a session limit and was re-run this session — it passed and corrected ZYN's can from 21 to 20 pouches; current product pages show 20, an older FAQ said 21.) PRICE-PER-MG is now a REAL derived metric = priceDkk ÷ (packCount × repStrengthMg of the priced SKU). Prices are single-can LIST prices (multibuy is materially cheaper per can) and are each shop's listed price — cross-shop variation is ~±10% (e.g. Snusbolaget lists ~4 kr above Haypp SE), so treat as representative, not the single universal price. DKK conversions use ECB cross-rates (GBP→DKK 8.72, SEK→DKK 0.66, DKK 1:1 — see src/data/feeds/ecb-fx.json). Online bestseller RANK is no longer surfaced as a claim (it was never separately sourced); the rank field now only orders rows. COVERAGE (disclosed, not silently omitted): the board tracks XQS (STG), VELO (BAT), ZYN (PMI) and Nordic Spirit (JTI); ZYN is included in Denmark. Nordic Spirit's Denmark availability post-cap is UNVERIFIED, so it is deliberately left out of the DK board rather than faked. On! (Altria) and smaller brands are not yet tracked. This was a manual owner-authorised snapshot, NOT the gated automated crawler (scripts/crawl-radar.ts stays gated). The per-market XQS SKU grids that the same snapshot captured — the input for the P2 regulatory-exposure band — live in src/data/radar/xqs-skus.json.

sources.name: Haypp Nicotine Pouch Report 2025
sources.use: UK market structure + leaderboard (VELO #1, Nordic Spirit #2; mint = 58% of cans) — the strongest public spec source
sources.tos: Free published report — used as a cited source, not crawled.

sources.name: Manufacturer & retailer product listings
sources.use: per-brand strengths, flavour ranges & pack counts (zyn.com, northerner, snusdaddy, thesnusoutlet, sik.dk for the DK cap)
sources.tos: Public product-spec pages — curated by hand + dated, never request-time scraped. UK HIGH / SE-DK MED confidence.

sources.name: Owner-cleared price snapshot (2026-06-17)
sources.use: real single-can list prices per market — UK via nicotine-pouches.org/Haypp UK; SE via Haypp SE + Snusbolaget; DK via din-ecigaret.dk +2 shops. Feeds the real price-per-mg.
sources.tos: One-time owner-authorised snapshot, not a live crawl. UK, DK and SE prices each independently cross-verified against a 2nd shop (SE via Nettotobak — exact 1-pack matches). scripts/crawl-radar.ts stays gated.

markets.name: Sweden
markets.note: World's largest pouch market. XQS is #2 (share 7.8%→13.6% since 2024).

markets.name: United Kingdom
markets.note: XQS focus market — sales doubled in Q4 2025; convenience channel fastest-growing.

markets.name: Denmark
markets.note: 9 mg/pouch cap + tobacco/menthol-only flavours in force Apr 2026 — flavour-led SKUs delisted.

prices.flavourStyle: fruit/fusion (Cola Lime, Fizzy Peach) + mint hedge

prices.priceNote: listed 35 kr; both corroborating shops run ~39 kr, so 35 is at the low end

events.text: Denmark 9 mg cap + tobacco/menthol-only flavours in force — XQS flavour-led SKUs delisted in DK.
France oral-nicotine ban in force — France removed from XQS's expansion map.
XQS Sweden share 12.3% (Q4 2025) → 13.6% (Q1 2026), up from 7.8% in 2024; XQS volume +38% vs +21% category.
XQS entering the mint segment — an explicit hedge against flavour regulation.
STG discontinued third-party ZYN distribution (NAOR) — drove NGP organic −17%.
